thesis: Qualcomm is a high-quality, cash-generative wireless semiconductor and licensing leader with a durable 5G/SoC moat and expanding exposure to AI PCs, automotive, and IoT. At ~$185 and ~20.6x P/E, the stock is fairly valued with balanced risk/reward; upside depends on the Snapdragon X Elite AI PC ramp, automotive design wins, and a steady smartphone refresh cycle, while downside risks include Apple modem insourcing, China exposure, and licensing disputes.

drivers: Strong 5G Android flagship refresh cycle lifts Snapdragon ASPs and unit share
Snapdragon X Elite captures meaningful Windows Copilot+ PC share, driving a new compute revenue stream
Automotive digital cockpit and modem design wins ramp faster than expected
IoT and industrial chip demand recovers from the cyclical downturn
QTL licensing remains stable and supports margin expansion

risks: Apple accelerates internal modem development and reduces Qualcomm supply share
China handset demand weakens or geopolitical/export actions disrupt customer access
AI PC adoption disappoints and Snapdragon X Elite fails to gain traction against x86/Apple
Licensing disputes, antitrust actions, or customer renegotiations pressure QTL margins
Smartphone cyclical downturn resumes and compresses the P/E multiple to ~15–16x

base_case.assumptions: Smartphone demand stabilizes with modest 5G upgrade activity; AI PC begins to contribute but is not a near-term needle-mover; automotive and IoT grow steadily but do not fully offset mobile maturity; QTL remains high-margin and stable. Stock trades at ~20–21x forward earnings on ~$10.00–10.50 EPS.

catalysts.event: Fiscal Q3 2026 earnings — handset and automotive trajectory update
Fiscal Q4 2026 earnings — AI PC Snapdragon X Elite ramp and FY2026 guidance
Windows Copilot+ PC holiday demand and OEM design-win data
FY2027 guidance and capital allocation update

key_risks: China handset market exposure and geopolitical/export control risk
Smartphone cyclicality and lengthening replacement cycles
AI PC adoption slower than hoped for Snapdragon X Elite
Licensing disputes, antitrust actions, and customer renegotiations
